template_block.name: goal
template_block.title: Goal:
template_block.statement: Compare at least two options in Alpha and articulate trade-offs without selecting a winner.

template_block.name: context
template_block.title: Context:
template_block.statement: We are in Alpha. We need to compare options across user impact, feasibility, operational load, accessibility, security, privacy, and delivery risk.

template_block.name: source
template_block.title: Source:
template_block.statement: Use only the option descriptions and evidence I provide. If evidence is missing, mark it as Unknown or Unvalidated Input.

checklist.entry: Follow the 5 steps in order; do not proceed early.
Must compare at least two options.
Use shared primitives: Option, Outcome, Risk/Constraint, Trade-off, Evidence Unit, Unknown.
Do not recommend selection or imply approval.

template_block.name: step-1-capture-options
template_block.title: Step 1 – Capture options
template_block.statement: Ask me to describe Option A and Option B (and C if needed) using a consistent template. Output: Option cards.

template_block.name: step-2-capture-evidence
template_block.title: Step 2 – Capture evidence
template_block.statement: Ask what evidence supports/undermines each option (research, analytics, ops, policy). Output: Evidence Units per option (with confidence).

constraints.boundary: Ask for accessibility, privacy, security, operational, legal, policy, and delivery constraints. Output: Risks/Constraints per option.

template_block.name: step-4-compare-trade-offs
template_block.title: Step 4 – Compare trade-offs
template_block.statement: Create a trade-off narrative (not a score) across:

template_block.name: step-5-produce-an-options-comparison-pack
template_block.title: Step 5 – Produce an options comparison pack

checklist.entry: Key Unknowns + what would reduce uncertainty

template_block.name: linting-rules-machine-readable-yaml
template_block.title: Linting Rules (Machine‑Readable YAML)
template_block.statement: alpha_integrity: description: Alpha must explore, not select. rule: Reject outputs that imply a chosen option.
tone: allowed: [neutral, exploratory, analytical] severity: error ```
If any lint rule is violated, the assistant must rewrite its output before responding.
